channel.title: manual on ecmccfg
channel.description: Recent content in manual on ecmccfg

item.title: build at PSI
item.description: These instructions only work at PSI! Build at PSI, using driver.makefile by default this module is only built for Debian 10 and 12 and Epics >=R7.0.7 build on login cluster make [LIBVERSION] [clean] [uninstall] install check a particular version check the module loads for version dev, Epics R7+ and ECMC version dev, note MASTER_ID=-1 which runs ECMC in master-less mode. Make proper adjustments to match your needs in terms of versions.

item.title: Quickstart
item.description: Goal Bring up an IOC with a single axis using the YAML-based configuration. Prerequisites hugo/Readme.md steps completed (repo cloned, prerequisites installed) Working EtherCAT master and reachable slaves One YAML parser backend: jinja (default): Python 3 with jinja2 (auto-installed by the axis loader) ecb: C++ backend (ECMC_CFG_TOOL=ecb, see ecb) Steps Load the module version you want: require ecmccfg <optional VERSION> Add the coupler and the relevant slaves (example: coupler + EL1018 + EL7062): ${SCRIPTEXEC} ${ecmccfg_DIR}addSlave.

item.title: introduction
item.description: Principle EtherCAT requires the field bus components (slaves) to be configured on the master. The master must know about the data exchanges with the slaves, this is referred to as process image. During IOC-startup, the requested configuration is validated against the actually present hardware on the bus. Mismatches will result in an error, the IOC will not start. Blindly restarting the IOC with only partially working EtherCAT hardware will result in an inoperable IOC!

item.title: best practice
item.description: Links to Best Practice General Motion PLC ECMC Core Notes (From ecmc Release Docs) For recent core changes and recommendations, see the ecmc release notes (for example RELEASE.md in the ecmc repo). Highlights to consider in your configurations: Use native axis auto-enable/disable (axis.autoEnable), added in ecmc v11. Keep SDO verification enabled to catch drive current/SDO mismatches before startup. You can declare PLC variables with VAR ... END_VAR blocks for clearer PLC code.

item.title: Examples
item.description: Reference scenarios (PSI best practice) Motion (examples/PSI/best_practice/motion/) Stepper BiSS-C (EL7041/EL5042, EL7062/EL5042) Stepper incremental Stepper open loop + retries Stepper without motor record Servo CSV Hardware substitution template Synchronization (examples/PSI/best_practice/motion/syncs/) Slit synchronization Mirror synchronization PLC examples (examples/PSI/best_practice/plcs/) Toggle PLC and template Common scripts addSlave.cmd, applyComponent.cmd: add slaves and apply drive/encoder components loadYamlAxis.cmd: load axis YAML (physical/virtual) configureVirtualAxis.cmd: classic virtual axis config (legacy) loadPLCFile.

item.title: Upgrades
item.description: Purpose Collect upgrade notes and migration checklists for major versions. Release Notes New features are described in ecmc RELEASE.md: https://github.com/epics-modules/ecmc/blob/master/RELEASE.md v10 -> v11 Highlights CSP-PC mode added (centralized position loop in CSP) Position-velocity-time (profile move) support Motor record PID fields still limited to 0..1.0; use values 100x smaller than native ecmc settings Be careful when using motor record PID fields. Example: ecmc Kp=1.0 corresponds to motor record PCOF=0.01.
